other_observers: None.; John Tumasonis and several others on 20 May.
specific_location: Crom Lake. About a quarter mile south of Road 90 on Road 31.
description: Smallish brown and white duck, smaller than nearby Gadwall; head was mostly a rich brown except for a large white eye patch that extended from near the bill widely around the eye then tapering to a point; the short, flat bill had a slight pinkish wash to the posterior half; the upperparts were mostly the same brown as the head except for some pale edging to the feathers; the underparts I could see were purely white; there was a slight brownish wash to the lower neck region.Behaviors: Swimming with Gadwall and Mallards.Call: None heard.Plumage: transitional into adult alternate
